reforms.description: The Danish government introduced a new scheme allowing firms that spend on R&D and face after-tax income losses to benefit from a tax refund of 25% of these losses, aimed at improving access to funding for young and financially constrained firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This reform enhances financial support for R&D activities, particularly for young firms, thereby fostering innovation and productivity growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The scheme was introduced to support R&D spending among firms.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant change in the support structure for R&D, particularly for small and young firms, which is crucial for fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: In 2012, a new tax incentives scheme was introduced, allowing firms that spend on R&D and face after-tax income losses to benefit from a tax refund of 25% of these losses.

reforms.description: The Danish Innovation Strategy launched in December 2012 includes initiatives to encourage cooperation between businesses and educational institutions through public-private partnerships.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering collaboration between businesses and educational institutions, this initiative aims to enhance knowledge transfer and innovation outcomes.
reforms.status_evidence: The Danish Innovation Strategy includes various initiatives to encourage cooperation between business and education institutions.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant effort to enhance collaboration between sectors, which is crucial for driving innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The Danish Innovation Strategy that was launched by the government in December 2012 includes various initiatives to encourage cooperation between business and education institutions.

reforms.description: Denmark is moving towards a more balanced mix of project and institutional-based research funding to enhance links between universities and industry.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving the connection between academia and industry can lead to increased innovation and productivity in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The recommendation suggests a shift in funding strategy to enhance collaboration.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in funding strategy that could enhance innovation capacity through better collaboration.
reforms.source_quote: Move towards a more balanced mix of project and institutional based research funding with the objective to increase the links between universities and industry.

reforms.description: Denmark has implemented a strategy to promote public-private partnerships (PPPs) to enhance competition for public sector contracts and improve cooperation between public authorities and private companies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing cooperation between public and private sectors can lead to more efficient public services and stimulate innovation in the private sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The Council for Public-Private Co-operation was established in April 2013 to support this initiative.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant effort to enhance the role of the private sector in public procurement, which can lead to improved service delivery and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: In 2011, a strategy to promote public-private cooperation was initiated.

reforms.description: In December 2012, the Danish government launched an Innovation Strategy that identified gaps in the innovation framework and proposed initiatives to enhance Denmark's position as an innovation leader, focusing on societal challenges and improving knowledge capacity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The strategy aims to enhance innovation capacity and productivity by addressing societal challenges and improving the efficiency of innovation policies.
reforms.status_evidence: The government launched an Innovation Strategy with various initiatives.
reforms.importance_rationale: The strategy represents a comprehensive approach to enhancing Denmark's innovation ecosystem, with significant implications for productivity and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: In December 2012, the government launched an Innovation Strategy. It identified some gaps in the innovation framework and proposed initiatives to help Denmark remain an innovation leader.

reforms.description: In 2014, Denmark plans to merge three different funding bodies into the Innovation Foundation to improve the efficiency and transparency of public support for basic research.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The merger aims to streamline funding processes, reduce red tape, and enhance the effectiveness of public research funding.
reforms.status_evidence: The three different funding bodies will be merged into the Innovation Foundation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the funding landscape for research, aiming to enhance efficiency and effectiveness in public R&D support.
reforms.source_quote: In 2014, the three different funding bodies will be merged into the Innovation Foundation with a view to improve efficiency, increase transparency and reduce red tape.

reforms.description: A new cluster strategy was launched to improve framework conditions for innovation and knowledge sharing, focusing on expanding clusters and increasing collaboration at various levels.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhances collaboration and knowledge sharing, which can lead to increased innovation and competitiveness among SMEs.
reforms.status_evidence: The new cluster strategy was launched in April 2013.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change aimed at enhancing innovation through improved cluster dynamics, which is crucial for the competitiveness of the Danish economy.
reforms.source_quote: In April 2013, a new cluster strategy was launched to improve framework conditions for innovation and knowledge sharing, with a view to expanding clusters.

reforms.description: The government is evaluating the recent merger of different innovation funding programmes to streamline innovation policy instruments and funding programmes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Streamlining funding programmes can enhance efficiency and effectiveness in supporting innovation, potentially leading to increased economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The recommendation to evaluate and streamline innovation funding programmes was made.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, this reform is more about improving existing structures rather than creating new ones, thus it is important but not major.
reforms.source_quote: Evaluate the recent merger of different innovation funding programmes and if needed, consider further streamlining innovation policy instruments and funding programmes.

reforms.description: Denmark launched an Innovation Strategy in December 2012, comprising 27 initiatives aimed at creating new jobs and growth through enhanced research, innovation, and education.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The strategy aims to foster innovation and job creation, which can enhance productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Innovation Strategy was launched with specific initiatives.
reforms.importance_rationale: The strategy represents a comprehensive approach to enhancing innovation across multiple sectors, which is crucial for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: In December 2012, the Ministry of Science, Innovation and Higher Education launched an Innovation Strategy, with a view to create new jobs and growth through 27 initiatives in the areas of research, innovation and education.

reforms.description: The restructuring of the 'Business Innovation Fund' into a 'Market Development Fund' aims to support the introduction of innovative solutions to the market, including green and welfare solutions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating the market introduction of innovative solutions, the fund is expected to enhance competitiveness and drive economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The restructuring of the Business Innovation Fund was part of the announced Innovation Strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant shift in funding focus, aiming to better align public support with market needs.
reforms.source_quote: Restructure the 'Business Innovation Fund' to turn it into a 'Market Development Fund' to support the introduction of innovative solutions to the market, including green and welfare solutions.

reforms.description: Denmark aims to enhance the efficiency of its innovation policies by ensuring that some schemes provide effective support to young and dynamic innovative firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving support for innovative firms can lead to increased productivity and competitiveness in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The recommendation emphasizes the need for better support mechanisms for innovative firms.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses a significant aspect of the innovation policy framework, focusing on enhancing support for a key segment of the economy.
reforms.source_quote: It is important to make sure that some schemes provide efficient support to young and dynamic innovative firms.

reforms.description: The OECD recommends extending carry-over provisions and cash refunds in R&D tax credit programmes or increasing direct support to better assist young and dynamic firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing R&D tax credits can incentivize firms to invest more in research and development, leading to innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The recommendation suggests improvements to R&D tax credit programmes to support innovative firms.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it directly impacts the financial incentives for firms to engage in R&D activities.
reforms.source_quote: Further extend carry-over provisions and cash refunds in R&D tax credit programmes or increase direct support.

reforms.description: The Danish government established five Business Development Centres (Vaeksthus) in 2007 to promote entrepreneurship and support high-growth firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These centres aim to enhance the entrepreneurial environment and provide tailored support to high-growth firms, which can lead to increased innovation and economic dynamism.
reforms.status_evidence: The Business Development Centres were established to support entrepreneurship.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of these centres represents a significant effort to enhance support for entrepreneurship, although it is part of a broader initiative rather than a standalone major reform.
reforms.source_quote: In 2007, five Business Development Centres (Vaeksthus) were established to promote entrepreneurship and high-growth firms.

reforms.description: In April 2013, the state grant system for tertiary education was reformed to speed up completion and thereby raise labour supply, introducing stricter conditions for grant eligibility and shortening the average duration of study programmes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By incentivizing quicker completion of tertiary education, the reform aims to increase the skilled labor supply, which can enhance productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform was enacted in April 2013.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of the grant system, aiming to improve educational outcomes and labor market readiness, which is crucial for economic competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: In April 2013, the state grant system for tertiary education was reformed to speed up completion and thereby raise labour supply.
